description: P1-N11 — AUDITED QField layer naming patterns extracted from real SIG_[project] data structures. Replaces theoretical patterns with factual layer names, attributes, and schemas from actual projects.

core_discovery_patterns.description: Layer types found consistently across all audited projects, with actual GPKG filenames and layer names

observations.display_name: Observations & Site Notes (Monitored conditions, issues, findings)
observations.usage: Primary layer for field observations, monitoring notes, issues & findings. Synced bidirectionally with QField cloud.

infrastructure_linear.display_name: Linear Infrastructure (Water systems, roads, utilities, irrigation)
infrastructure_linear.usage: Complex infrastructure inventory with full engineering specifications

infrastructure_points.display_name: Infrastructure Points (Access points, connection nodes, junctions)

core_attributes.type: String (point type: junction, access, connection, etc.)

infrastructure_points.usage: Infrastructure junction and access point details

zoning_planning.display_name: Zoning & Management Planning (Master zone definitions and strategies)
zoning_planning.usage: Master zoning matrix for landscape management planning. Often multiple versions for scenario planning.

interventions.usage: Planting locations, intervention lines, work installations, tree rows

vegetation_inventory.display_name: Vegetation Inventory (Tree database with spectral analysis)
vegetation_inventory.usage: Detailed tree inventory with spectral analysis, health metrics, species tracking
vegetation_inventory.species_reference: PlantList_FullList layer contains 234 species master data

access_paths.display_name: Access & Path Network (Routes and connectivity infrastructure)

core_attributes.type: String (path type: walking, service, maintenance, etc.)

access_paths.usage: Access route network with surface specs, width, slopes, accessibility features

points_of_interest.display_name: Points of Interest (Notable locations, landmarks, viewpoints)
points_of_interest.usage: Visitor attractions, landmarks, viewpoints, interpretive locations

property_boundary.usage: Property perimeter boundary, overall project study area extent

elevation_reference.usage: Reference elevation points for DEM validation and topographic context

core_attributes.geometry: original cadastral property polygons from government registry

cadastral_data.note: Project-specific - check for presence before loading. Not present in all projects.
cadastral_data.usage: Cadastral property boundaries for legal/administrative context

backward_compatibility_mappings.note: Alternate GPKG filenames and layer names that may appear in older projects or different naming schemes

aggregation_virtual_layers.description: Virtual layers created by OFFICE-QFIELD-WATCHER for EPT admin dashboard (P1-N12)

all_observations.display_name: All Observations (Multi-geometry union across all projects)

all_infrastructure.display_name: All Infrastructure (Linear + Points union across all projects)

project_activity_metrics.calculation: Feature counts by layer type, last_modified timestamps, data quality metrics, sync status

qfield_cloud_patterns.note: QField cloud folder patterns to monitor

qfield_cloud_sync_patterns.description: Sync patterns discovered from audit of rectificacao_-_quinta_das_artosas QField cloud project
findings: QField cloud projects are mirrors of SIG_[project] with added features/fields during field work
Notas.gpkg schemas are IDENTICAL between cloud and base project
Only difference is feature count (cloud has newly collected features)
All other core GeoPackages are present with same structure
Photo galleries are synced bidirectionally (cloud ↔ project)
Layer order and naming convention strictly preserved
qfield_cloud_sync_patterns.sync_model: 2-way bidirectional with cloud as primary data source during field work
qfield_cloud_sync_patterns.conflict_resolution: Cloud version takes precedence; local project merges in photo updates
qfield_cloud_sync_patterns.feature_sync_direction: Cloud → Project after field work completion
qfield_cloud_sync_patterns.attribute_sync: All fields preserved; new attributes added by cloud are merged back

project_structure_template.description: Standard directory and file structure for new SIG_[project] ensuring automation compatibility

required_vector_files.observations: Notas.gpkg (Pontos Notaveis, Linhas Notaveis, Areas Notaveis, notas_photo_gallery)
required_vector_files.vegetation: Arvores.gpkg (Árvores, Arvores_proposta, Tree Photo Gallery, PlantList_FullList)
required_vector_files.interventions: Linha de Plantacao.gpkg (Linha de Plantacao, Linha de Plantacao_photo_gallery)

project_structure_template.qgis_project_file: Projeto QGIS.qgs (main QGIS project with layer styles, forms, and layouts)
initialization_checklist: 1. Create all required directories per template
2. Copy project_vector_data/*.gpkg from template SIG_Artosas or latest project
3. Update property_boundaries.gpkg with new property boundary polygon
4. Edit Projeto QGIS.qgs to update project title, layer sources, and data paths
5. Verify all layer CRS = EPSG:3763 (Portugal TM06)
6. Run project_orchestrator --validate to check structure compliance
7. Create QField cloud project pointing to new SIG_[project]
8. Test sync with first field data collection
project_structure_template.crs_requirement: EPSG:3763 (ETRS89 / Portugal TM06) for all vector layers

layer_discovery_priority_order: 7. CORE: interventions (Linha de Plantacao.gpkg) - Always present
11. OPTIONAL: points_of_interest (Pontos de Interesse.gpkg) - Check for presence

critical_field_tracking.uuid_field: REQUIRED in all layers for bidirectional sync with QField cloud
critical_field_tracking.master_field: Present in most layers for feature classification
critical_field_tracking.name_field: Standard naming across all observation/infrastructure layers
critical_field_tracking.photo_galleries: Paired *_photo_gallery layers provide QField sync mechanism

validation_requirements: All layers must load with CRS EPSG:3763
All feature geometries must be valid (no self-intersections, null geometries, etc.)
All layers must include uuid field for sync tracking
Photo galleries must be linked to parent layers for QField editing
Virtual layers aggregation must preserve attribute schemas for EPT dashboard
sync_workflow: 1. OFFICE-QFIELD-WATCHER monitors QField cloud for new features/fields
2. Photo galleries trigger automatic syncs via photo_gallery_layer updates
3. New features propagate from cloud → SIG_Project via Python uuid matching
4. Attributes merged via schema matching (new fields auto-added to project layers)
5. Conflicts resolved: cloud version takes precedence for new data
6. Sync completeness verified via feature count and field comparison

schema_consistency.photo_galleries: ✓ Paired galleries present for each main layer

qfield_cloud_sync.schema_comparison: ✓ Cloud schemas match SIG_Project base schemas
qfield_cloud_sync.feature_count_variance: ✓ Cloud has additional features (field work data)
